is an American [hip hop](/wiki/Hip_hop "Hip hop") duo, formed in 1995, which hails from [The Bronx](/wiki/The_Bronx "The Bronx"), [New York](/wiki/New_York_(state) "New York (state)").[[1]](#cite_note-LarkinDM-1) The duo consists of [rappers](/wiki/Rapping "Rapping") Sonny Cheeba (Salahadeen Wilds) and Geechi Suede (Saladine Wallace).[[2]](#cite_note-2) The duo is perhaps best known for their 1997 hit "[Luchini AKA This Is It](/wiki/Luchini_AKA_This_Is_It "Luchini AKA This Is It")".

American hip hop group

**Camp Lo** is an American [hip hop](/wiki/Hip_hop "Hip hop") duo, formed in 1995, which hails from [The Bronx](/wiki/The_Bronx "The Bronx"), [New York](/wiki/New_York_(state) "New York (state)").[[1]](#cite_note-LarkinDM-1) The duo consists of [rappers](/wiki/Rapping "Rapping") Sonny Cheeba (Salahadeen Wilds) and Geechi Suede (Saladine Wallace).[[2]](#cite_note-2) The duo is perhaps best known for their 1997 hit "[Luchini AKA This Is It](/wiki/Luchini_AKA_This_Is_It "Luchini AKA This Is It")".

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Camp_Lo&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Career")]

Initially signed with [Profile Records](/wiki/Profile_Records "Profile Records"), Camp Lo made their first appearance in 1996 on *[The Great White Hype](/wiki/The_Great_White_Hype "The Great White Hype")* soundtrack, with their [single](/wiki/Single_(music) "Single (music)"), "Coolie High". The smooth party song was a minor hit, peaking at No. 25 on the *[Billboard](/wiki/Billboard_(magazine) "Billboard (magazine)")* [Hot Rap Singles](/wiki/Hot_Rap_Tracks "Hot Rap Tracks") chart. Their first major exposure came with the release of their second single, the upbeat, horn-driven "Luchini", otherwise known as "This Is It". The song became a crossover hit in 1997, breaking into the Top 50 on the [*Billboard* Hot 100](/wiki/Billboard_Hot_100 "Billboard Hot 100"), and reaching the top 5 on the Hot Rap Singles chart. The track peaked at No. 74 in the [UK Singles Chart](/wiki/UK_Singles_Chart "UK Singles Chart") in August 1997.[[3]](#cite_note-British_Hit_Singles_&_Albums-3) The duo released their debut album *[Uptown Saturday Night](/wiki/Uptown_Saturday_Night_(album) "Uptown Saturday Night (album)")* in January 1997.[[1]](#cite_note-LarkinDM-1) The effort was widely acclaimed, praised for its accessible content and smooth, [funk](/wiki/Funk "Funk")- and [jazz](/wiki/Jazz "Jazz")-inspired production.[[1]](#cite_note-LarkinDM-1)

The majority of the album was produced by [Ski](/wiki/Ski_(producer) "Ski (producer)"), famous for his work with [Jay-Z](/wiki/Jay-Z "Jay-Z").[[1]](#cite_note-LarkinDM-1) Guest appearances were provided by [De La Soul](/wiki/De_La_Soul "De La Soul")'s Trugoy and [Digable Planets](/wiki/Digable_Planets "Digable Planets")' Butterfly.[[1]](#cite_note-LarkinDM-1) The album's 1970s styled content was influential on a number of artists within the next few years after its release, like [Will Smith](/wiki/Will_Smith "Will Smith"), who enlisted Camp Lo for an appearance on his top-selling *[Big Willie Style](/wiki/Big_Willie_Style "Big Willie Style")* album. Also in 1997, they appeared on [Oran "Juice" Jones](/wiki/Oran_%22Juice%22_Jones "Oran \"Juice\" Jones")' single "Poppin' That Fly". They collaborated with [De La Soul](/wiki/De_La_Soul "De La Soul") in 2000, on the song "So Good" from the *Hip Hop 101 Compilation*. They finally returned with another album in 2002, titled *[Let's Do It Again](/wiki/Let%27s_Do_It_Again_(Camp_Lo_album) "Let's Do It Again (Camp Lo album)")*. This effort was not nearly as well-received as their debut, and was met with mediocre reviews and sales. Shortly after the album's release, [Aesop Rock](/wiki/Aesop_Rock "Aesop Rock") enlisted the duo to appear on his album *[Bazooka Tooth](/wiki/Bazooka_Tooth "Bazooka Tooth")*, exposing them to fans of the underground [Definitive Jux](/wiki/Definitive_Jux "Definitive Jux") label. In 2007, the duo returned with *[Black Hollywood](/wiki/Black_Hollywood "Black Hollywood")* after a five-year hiatus from their previous release.

In April 2008, Camp Lo signed with SRC/Universal Records and changed its name to the Lo, on the recommendation from DJ Mr. Cee. The duo released a new single titled "Lumdi",[[4]](#cite_note-4) [produced](/wiki/Record_producer "Record producer") by Korleon.[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6) They also confirmed a 2011 release for a new collaboration album with [Pete Rock](/wiki/Pete_Rock "Pete Rock"), as they form their new group '80 Blocks From Tiffanys'.

In 2012, Geechi Suede collaborated with TheeKIDICARUS on a song "Ask About Me" where TheeKIDICARUS would produce the track.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

In 2013, Geechi Suede was featured on the song "21st Century" from *#TheRevival*, a compilation album by Philly-based rappers/producers Franz Kafka and The TradeMark Experience.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

In May 2015, Camp Lo released a song titled "Black Jesus" via [2DOPEBOYZ](/wiki/2DOPEBOYZ "2DOPEBOYZ") in anticipation for their 5th album titled *Ragtime Hightimes*.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] On May 18, 2015, Camp Lo released their album *Ragtime Hightimes*.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

In 2016, Sonny Cheeba featured on [The Avalanches](/wiki/The_Avalanches "The Avalanches")' song "Because I'm Me" on the album *[Wildflower](/wiki/Wildflower_(The_Avalanches_album) "Wildflower (The Avalanches album)")*.[[7]](#cite_note-7)

On September 22, 2017, Camp Lo released the first of three *A Piece of the Action* albums series, leading up to their sixth album titled *The Get Down Brothers*.[[8]](#cite_note-8)
